forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -51,4 +51,4 @@
 ### Nits
 - [x] `kanban-cli/src/logging.rs` (tests module) ‚Äî The `file_writer_guard_flushes_on_write` test exercises `swissarmyhammer_common::logging::FileWriterGuard`, which is already covered by `write_flushes_to_disk` in `swissarmyhammer-common/src/logging.rs`. Since this crate imports `FileWriterGuard` rather than redefining it, the local test duplicates upstream coverage and doesn't exercise anything this module owns. Consider replacing it with a test for `init_tracing` ‚Äî e.g., a test (with `#[serial]` to avoid clobbering the global subscriber) that confirms `.kanban/` is not auto-created when absent, and that `.kanban/mcp.log` is created when the directory exists.
 - [x] `kanban-cli/src/logging.rs` `init_tracing` ‚Äî When `.kanban/` exists but `File::create` fails (permissions, disk full, read-only fs), the function silently falls back to stderr. The `sah-cli` analogue emits a `eprintln!("Warning: Could not setup MCP logging: {}. Falling back to stderr.", e)` in that case. Consider matching that behavior so users aren't surprised when logs don't appear in the expected file.
-- [x] `kanban-cli/src/main.rs` ‚Äî `logging::init_tracing(false)` hardcodes `debug = false`. The `debug` parameter is never exercised at any call site. This is acceptable as a placeholder since future cards will wire a CLI flag, but consider adding a `// TODO:` comment noting that a `--debug` / `-v` flag is pending so the wiring isn't forgotten.
+- [x] `kanban-cli/src/main.rs` ‚Äî `logging::init_tracing(false)` hardcodes `debug = false`. The `debug` parameter is never exercised at any call site. This is acceptable as a placeholder since future cards will wire a CLI flag, but consider adding a `// TODO:` comment noting that a `--debug` / `-v` flag is pending so the wiring isn't forgotten.
\ No newline at end of file
